516.reason: [When an ILLEGAL token is encountered (e.g., single '&' or '|'), the program still outputs it to stdout and lexer.txt. It should skip output for ILLEGAL tokens, since errors should only go to error.txt]

517.reason: After detecting an error (e.g., ILLEGAL token from single '&' or '|'), the program should stop outputting to lexer.txt and only output to error.txt. This line incorrectly outputs tokens even when an error has been reported, causing both outputs to be generated instead of only the error output.

reasons.buggy-claude-sonnet-4-6-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: ILLEGAL tokens should not be written to lexer.txt. The condition should also check lexer.getLexType() != LexType::ILLEGAL before writing output]
